nodes.context: First known and oldest New York City criminal street gang, formed in 1825, primarily Irish immigrants and Irish Americans, active in Five Points.
Another criminal gang named the Forty Thieves formed in London, England in 1828, with no criminal ties to the New York gang.
All-female London criminal gang active from 1873-1950s, also known to use the name the Forty Thieves.
A criminal gang in Philadelphia that called themselves the Forty Thieves.
Another early Irish gang formed in the same year as the Forty Thieves, alleged to be the second oldest organized criminal gang in New York City.
An apprentice street gang of the original Forty Thieves, active in the 1850s.
Ally of the Forty Thieves as per infobox.
Ally of the Forty Thieves as per infobox.
Ally of the Forty Thieves as per infobox.
Corrupt bureaucracy that provided community services in exchange for money and support; established relations with the Forty Thieves.
Rival of the Forty Thieves as per infobox.
Rival of the Forty Thieves as per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Another criminal gang named the "Forty Thieves" which had no criminal ties to the New York gang was formed in London , England in 1828.
edges.context: The text explicitly states there were no criminal ties between the two gangs.

edges.evidence_quote: From 1873-1950s, an all-female London criminal gang known as the " Forty Elephants " was also known to use the name the Forty Thieves.
edges.context: The Forty Elephants used the same name but no criminal ties are mentioned.

edges.evidence_quote: The Kerryonians , another early Irish gang formed in the same year as the Forty Thieves, have been alleged to be the second oldest organized criminal gang in New York City.
edges.context: Both formed in 1825, but no direct relationship is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: The juvenile Little Forty Thieves , an apprentice street gang of the original Forty Thieves, would outlast their mentors, continuing to commit illegal activities throughout the 1850s before eventually joining the later street gangs following the American Civil War in 1865.
edges.context: Little Forty Thieves was an apprentice gang of the original Forty Thieves.

edges.evidence_quote: The Forty Thieves saw this as an economic opportunity, as they established relations with Tammany Hall.
edges.context: The Forty Thieves established relations with Tammany Hall, a corrupt bureaucracy.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Chichesters , Shirt Tails , Kerryonians , Dead Rabbits , Tammany Hall
edges.context: Chichesters listed as an ally in the infobox.
